summary: Independent QA accepts M5-TASK-GRAPH-001. The review verified the actual chore/m5-task-graph diff at e9786544, the systems_architect REVIEW handoff, and the M5 task graph. M5-TASK-GRAPH-001 is REVIEW, all 9 downstream M5 tasks remain DRAFT, the graph is acyclic with existing dependencies, M5 formal passage and manual node battle remain explicit Human Gates, and no product/package/content/tool/CI/gate/roadmap/ADR/manifest paths changed. The graph is planning-only and does not pre-approve telemetry, server/multiplayer, arbitrary-code mods, release/commercial/branding decisions, paid services, frozen architecture changes, or the manual node battle implementation. M4_GATE remains PASS_WITH_LIMITS as an accepted process limit and is not restated as systems_architect acceptance.

No output before QA handoff creation.

decisions: The M5 task graph is dependency-ordered and acyclic: 10 M5 task files, 21 dependency edges, 20 internal M5 edges, and all dependencies exist in project/tasks/active.

All downstream M5 tasks remain DRAFT: slice definition, simulation loop, client playable UI, multi-year AI run, Web/Windows distributable evidence, performance gate, player research, manual node battle decision, and M5 exit Human Gate.
M5 formal passage remains a Human Gate in M5-EXIT-HUMAN-GATE-001 and must not be auto-passed by reviewer handoff or CI.
Manual node battle remains a separate Human Gate in M5-MANUAL-NODE-BATTLE-DECISION-001; the graph does not implement, approve, or silently route manual battle implementation.
Allowed and forbidden paths are scoped per task; future implementation tasks are still DRAFT and require their own writer/reviewer evidence before any product/package edits.

No current diff touches product code, package code, content-source data, tools, CI, prior gate docs, roadmap, acceptance matrix, ADRs, manifests, release checksum/report files, or dependency manifests.

Telemetry, server/multiplayer, arbitrary-code mods, release/commercial/branding, paid services, accounts/secrets, frozen architecture/platform/core changes, cultural-risk decisions, irreversible migrations, and manual node battle remain explicit exclusions or Human Gate stop conditions.

M4_GATE PASS_WITH_LIMITS is preserved as an accepted process limit caused by missing systems handoff after real stalled systems writer threads; this review found no wording that converts it into systems_architect acceptance.
risks: This ACCEPT covers only the planning DAG; it is not evidence that the playable slice, AI run, player research, performance gate, distributable smoke, or formal M5 passage exists.

Several downstream M5 tasks are R3/R4 because they will later touch deterministic authority state, protocol/save schemas, client read-model surfaces, Electron packaging/security, performance evidence, and Human Gate decisions.
M5 implementation must keep preserving M4 exclusions unless a later explicit Human Gate or ADR authorizes a change.
requested_action: Lead should integrate this QA ACCEPT handoff, run normal integration gates, and only then accept/close M5-TASK-GRAPH-001 or select the next dependency-ready M5 task. Do not mark M5 formally passed or start manual node battle implementation from this graph review.
